evidence: The word 'away' strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical separation from a boundary) and resonance.essential (core essence of exteriority). It also connects to perception (direct sensory awareness of external space), relational dynamics (boundary structures), and discovery (exploration beyond limits). Scores reflect its grounding in physical existence and existential contrast with 'inside.'

The word 'exterior' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and essential resonance (resonance.essential) as it fundamentally defines boundaries. It operates across scales from individual to stellar (scale.individual to scale.stellar) and connects deeply with relational dynamics (ontological.relational) due to its paired relationship with 'inside'.

The word 'exterior' strongly resonates with material existence (10), spatial boundaries at various scales (individual/community/stellar), and sensory perception (7). Its relational and structural aspects (5/4) reflect its contrast with 'inside,' while its catalytic role (3) ties to external influences. Aesthetic value (5) and functional utility (4) align with tangible exteriors in design or environments.

The word 'external' strongly resonates with 'resonance.essential' (10) as it directly embodies the core essence of SPC.outside. It scores highly in 'ontological.material' (5) because external often refers to physical entities. 'ontological.structural' (4) reflects the boundary's organizational role. Lower scores reflect tangential connections, like aesthetic value (3) for exteriors' design or contingent lawfulness (2) depending on boundary conditions.

Externality strongly resonates with societal scale (5), relational ontology (5), ethical value (5), and transformative symmetry (3), reflecting its role in systemic interactions, moral implications, and structural patterns.

The word 'extraneous' strongly resonates with the semantic layer (meaning/relevance) and structural exclusion, as it denotes things outside essential boundaries. It also connects to categorization (identification), practical utility (functional value), and comprehension of irrelevance. Scores reflect its focus on meaning, structure, and context-dependent exclusion.

Extraneousness strongly resonates with identification (3) and semantic meaning (3) as it defines what lies outside a boundary. It also aligns with superficial resonance (3) as it often pertains to surface-level exclusion. Ontological structural (2) and informational (3) connections reflect its role in defining system boundaries. Ethical (2) and functional (2) values arise from decisions about inclusion/exclusion.

Extrinsic strongly resonates with perception (direct sensory awareness of external factors), material (physical nature of external elements), and functional value (practical utility of outside influences). Moderate scores reflect its role in catalyzing change, contingent conditions, and structural patterns. Scores remain low for slots tied to internal dynamics or abstract existential themes.

The word 'inaccurate' strongly resonates with semantic slots tied to information (ontological.informational), structural misalignment (resonance.structural), and destructive consequences (symmetry.destructive). It weakly connects to individual-scale errors (scale.individual), identification failures (epistemological.identification), and contingent conditions (lawfulness.contingent).

The word 'open' strongly resonates with the material and structural layers of existence (ontological.material/structural), the semantic meaning of exterior/interior opposition (ontological.semantic), and the aesthetic value of open spaces (value.aesthetic). It also reflects the existential necessity of an 'outside' as a counterpart to 'inside' (resonance.existential) and the predictable, necessary presence of boundaries (lawfulness.necessary).

The word 'open' (as the exterior) strongly resonates with direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and material existence (ontological.material). It connects deeply with its symmetric pair (interconnection.resonant) and has practical/aesthetic value (value_generation.functional/aesthetic). Lower scores reflect its lack of temporal dynamics or ethical implications.

Open air strongly resonates with permanence and continuation as it is a constant, enduring aspect of the exterior. Its planetary scale (stellar) and material basis (physical presence) are core. Perception and experience highlight its sensory immediacy. Aesthetic value is central to its appeal. Lawfulness predictability reflects its consistent presence beyond boundaries.

The word 'out' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it directly denotes physical exteriority. Resonance.essential (10) captures its core essence of being beyond a boundary. Epistemological aspects (perception, identification, understanding, experience) score 2 each due to their roles in categorizing and sensing the exterior. Ontological.semantic (5) reflects its meaning layer, while lawfulness.predictable (5) and value_generation.functional (5) highlight its predictable, practical utility in spatial contexts.

The word strongly resonates with material existence (10), direct sensory experience (9), and aesthetic value (9). It connects to ecosystems (8), independent existence (8), and cyclical patterns (5). Lower scores reflect its lack of inherent temporal dynamics or ethical connotations.

Outdoors strongly resonates with material existence (physical presence), permanence (enduring state), predictability (constant presence), and functional/aesthetic values (practical utility and beauty). It also aligns with ecosystem scale, essential resonance (core essence of exterior space), and the necessity of boundaries in environments.

Outdoorsy strongly resonates with individual preference (scale.individual), physical engagement (ontological.material), sensory experience of nature (epistemological.perception/experience), and the essential resonance of being outside (resonance.essential). It also aligns with aesthetic appreciation of natural beauty (value.aesthetic) and practical outdoor skills (value.functional).

The word 'outer' strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical exterior) and epistemological.perception (direct sensory awareness of the exterior). It also connects moderately with scale.cosmic (as in outer space) and value.aesthetic (outer appearances often valued aesthetically). Lawfulness.predictable reflects the expected nature of exterior boundaries.

The highest scores reflect 'outside's core physicality (ontological.material), its essential resonance with its symmetric pair 'inside' (resonance.essential), and its experiential/aesthetic dimensions (epistemological.perception/experience, value.aesthetic). Lower scores align with its contingent boundaries (lawfulness.contingent) and functional uses (value.functional).

The word 'outside' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material=10) and its essential opposition to 'inside' (resonance.essential=10). It is perceived through the senses (epistemological.perception=8) and valued aesthetically (value_generation.aesthetic=8). Its structural and semantic layers (ontological.structural=7, ontological.semantic=8) reflect its role in defining boundaries at individual and societal scales (scale.individual=5, scale.society=3).
